questions.specification: A mining company re-evaluates a site containing low-grade iron ore. Due to the development of a new, low-cost smelting process, the ore can now be extracted and sold for a profit. How is this specific iron ore deposit now classified in comparison to its previous status?

options.content: It has been reclassified from a reserve to a resource.
It is now considered a renewable energy source.
It has been reclassified from a resource to a reserve.
It is now classified as an inexhaustible resource.

questions.specification: In the context of sustainable design, why is solar energy classified as a **renewable** resource while natural gas is classified as **non-renewable**?

options.content: Solar energy can be identified in terms of quantity and quality.
Solar energy is replenished within a human timeframe, whereas natural gas takes millions of years to form.
Natural gas is found in the Earth's crust, while solar energy is captured from the atmosphere.
Natural gas is currently more technically and economically feasible to extract than solar energy.

questions.specification:
The following graph illustrates the power generation profiles of two different energy sources, Source A and Source B, recorded over a 48-hour period.

![Energy Generation Comparison](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ramen-ramen-ib-physics-new-54ada86d-6b78-4ec8-9427-c2c3fea9e7c6-1771929956947/f367ce1f-a1f5-49f8-810c-55029efe3978.jpeg)

Based on the performance data shown, which combination of energy sources is most likely represented by Source A and Source B?

options.content: Source A: Wind; Source B: Solar
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Both wind and solar exhibit intermittent characteristics, meaning neither would produce a perfectly steady horizontal line across a 48-hour period.

options.content: Source A: Geothermal; Source B: Wind
options.markscheme: Correct. Geothermal energy provides a stable base load with constant output (Source A), whereas wind power is highly intermittent and weather-dependent (Source B).

options.content: Source A: Solar; Source B: Nuclear
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Solar energy output is diurnal and fluctuating (B), while nuclear energy is typically run as a constant base-load source (A).

options.content: Source A: Tidal; Source B: Geothermal
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Tidal power is periodic (it changes with the tides), whereas geothermal is constant. This reverses the patterns shown in the graph.

questions.specification: A designer is selecting materials for a sustainable product and chooses bamboo. Which of the following justifies the classification of bamboo as a renewable resource?

options.content: It has the ability to be processed into a variety of high-performance composite materials
It is replenished by natural growth at a rate that is at least equal to the rate of harvest
It can be returned to the soil to decompose naturally at the end of the product's life
It is found in abundance across a wide range of equatorial and temperate climates

questions.specification: A mineral survey of a salt flat identifies a total of $2.4 \times 10^6$ tonnes of lithium. Due to current infrastructure costs and legal protections on the surrounding land, only $600,000$ tonnes are accessible for commercial extraction at a profit. Which classification best identifies the $600,000$ tonnes of lithium?

options.content: Resource
Sustainable yield
Reserve
Natural capital

questions.specification: A geological survey confirms the existence of a high-grade copper deposit in a remote mountainous region. However, due to the current lack of infrastructure and the low market price of copper, extraction is not yet profitable. In the context of resource management, how is this deposit categorized?

options.content: It is categorized as a proven reserve.
It is categorized as a perpetual supply.
It is categorized as a resource but not a reserve.
It is categorized as a renewable energy source.

questions.specification: A sharp increase in the global market price of lithium makes the extraction of certain low-concentration brine deposits economically viable for the first time. How is this change most accurately described in terms of resource classification?

options.content: A portion of the resource base has been converted into a reserve.
The total physical quantity of the resource base has increased.
A portion of the proven reserve has been downgraded to a resource.
The lithium has transitioned from a non-renewable to a renewable resource.
